mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly compressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC remains below 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows or positive returns exceed 55% when FEDFUNDS increases.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending economic contraction, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE increases by > 0.2 percentage points month-over-month
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in > 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.05) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, which drives corporate revenue expectations higher and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Joint positive deviations in RSAFS and HOUST fail to predict positive returns with an accuracy above 55% over a 60-day horizon.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in the index of industrial production reflects expanding manufacturing and utility output, which generates positive short-term market momentum and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The signal produces negative forward returns in > 50% of observations during early-cycle expansions or Rank IC falls below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index year-over-year growth surpasses 6.0%, markets price in aggressive central bank tightening to combat inflation, which compresses equity multiples and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 forward returns are positive in > 65% of months where CPIAUCSL YoY exceeds 6.0%, or the predictive power vanishes after controlling for FEDFUNDS changes.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in total nonfarm payrolls and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm robust labor market absorption, which supports consumer spending forecasts and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The combined signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark with a Sharpe ratio above 0.5 over a 20-day holding period.

mechanism_narrative: Quarter-over-quarter growth in real gross domestic product reflects broad-based economic expansion, which elevates corporate profit trajectories and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Quarterly GDP growth fails to predict 60-day forward returns with a Rank IC above 0.03, or positive returns occur in less than 50% of expansion quarters.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production exceeding 0.5% signals a sudden contraction in manufacturing activity, which triggers risk-off positioning and generates negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Market returns remain positive in > 60% of instances following a 0.5% monthly decline in INDPRO, or the signal loses predictive accuracy after 2020.
